idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
Discovery seed from prior strong SM90 matmul (tm128/wn128/s4) adapted to 16384x512x16384 to establish a validated custom baseline before profile-driven optimization.
Raised WARP_N to 256 and reduced STAGES to 2 to improve skinny-N bandwidth/occupancy; correctness passed but TFLOPS remained below the current best.
Applied -maxrregcount=128 to reduce register pressure and improve occupancy, but additional spill/latency overhead reduced end-to-end TFLOPS.
Reduced pipeline stages 4->3 to lower shared-memory/sync overhead on the iter001 base; kernel remained correct but throughput did not surpass current best.
Increased MATMUL_TILE_M from 128 to 256 to raise M-dimension reuse and amortize memory traffic, but measured throughput stayed below the current best.
Reduced MATMUL_WARP_N from 128 to 64 to cut register/synchronization pressure per CTA, but measured TFLOPS still did not exceed the current best kernel.
Enabled ptxas cache-all load policy (-dlcm=ca) to improve cache reuse on global loads, but measured performance remained below the current best.
Applied -maxrregcount=136 as a softer occupancy push than 128, but performance still stayed below the current best kernel.
Reduced TILE_K to 32 with SWIZ 64 to shrink stage footprint and shared-memory pressure, but runtime throughput remained below the current best.
Enabled --use_fast_math to reduce scalar overhead around tensor-core work, but measured TFLOPS still underperformed the current best.
Applied a light -maxrregcount=152 cap to ease register pressure with minimal spill risk, but throughput still failed to beat the active best kernel.
Tested intermediate WARP_N=96 to balance reuse and pressure between 128 and 64, but it regressed throughput below both variants and the current best.
Tried a wider WARP_N=160 tile for stronger RHS reuse and fewer N tiles, but this introduced additional pressure and regressed performance versus the best baseline.
Persistent 1p1c warpspec TILE_M=64 WARP_N=96 STAGES=3: matched cuBLAS 96-wide N tile but 64x96 compute tile too small, tile occupancy gain lost to insufficient arithmetic intensity
WARP_N=64 STAGES=2 to cut SMEM 192KB->82KB for 2 CTAs/SM but halving pipeline depth killed MMA/TMA overlap, net regression vs iter001 1p2c baseline
Choreo --hoist-offset flag on iter001 base: stmatrix incompatible with f32 accum (dropped), hoist-offset alone gives 210.3 vs 214.9 TFLOPS - marginal regression
1p2c WARP_N=96 STAGES=2 targeting 2 CTAs/SM: 104KB SMEM fits threshold but 2-stage pipeline halves TMA buffering depth causing MMA starvation, net regression 127T vs 214T

Direct global store from WGMMA accumulators (no output SMEM) to eliminate 8.1-way bank conflicts. Saved 64KB SMEM (196->132KB) but uncoalesced global scatter cost exceeds SMEM bank conflict savings. Net regression 204.2T vs 214.9T.
WARP_N=64 STAGES=4 direct global store targeting 2 CTAs/SM (96KB SMEM). Direct store eliminates output SMEM but uncoalesced global scatter at N=64 too expensive (worse than N=128). 117.7T vs 214.9T.
TBC CLUSTER_M 2 RHS TMA multicast to halve B bandwidth
1p1c persistent TILE_M=64 WARP_N=96 STAGES=4 104KB SMEM for 2 CTAs/SM occupancy
1p1c TILE_K=32 WARP_N=64 STAGES=3 40KB SMEM for very high CTA occupancy
M-major tile ordering keeps A-matrix rows hot in L2 cache across 4 N-column passes
WARP_N=64 STAGES=2 maxrregcount=80 for 2 CTAs/SM: register spilling cut WGMMA from 63% to 31%, SM occupancy unchanged at 14%
1p3c TILE_M=192 STAGES=2 M-major: TILE_M=192 does not divide M=16384, causing out-of-bounds writes; kernel output corrupted
WARP_N=256 STAGES=2 M-major: 2-stage pipeline not enough to hide 256K-loop latency vs 4-stage WARP_N=128
STAGES=3 M-major: fewer pipeline stages reduce latency hiding vs STAGES=4, K=256 iterations need deep pipeline
Add --hoist-offset --hoist-scale --ptx-barrier Choreo flags to iter023; ptx-barrier reduces CTA barrier stall (top stall 17246 samples, 32.6% of cycles)
STAGES=3 with ptx-barrier hoist flags; fewer prologue barriers but insufficient pipeline depth hurts DRAM latency hiding
--tma-cluster-aware flag added to iter030; negligible benefit for single-CTA kernel
Phase2 CU fix: move warpgroup_arrive() before inner iv_warp loop (once per K-tile not once per WGMMA); correctly pipelines all 4 WGMMA ops, reduces warpgroup_arrive stall serialization
Phase2: warpgroup_wait<1>() inside K-loop + warpgroup_wait<0>() after loop; overlaps WGMMA completion of tile K with TMA load of tile K+1, hiding compute latency; +52.6 TFLOPS breakthrough
warpgroup_wait<2>() inside K-loop; no improvement over wait<1> - WGMMA-TMA overlap already saturated
WARP_N=256 STAGES=2 with wgmma_arrive_once+wait<1>; STAGES=2 insufficient pipeline depth hurts more than WARP_N=256 helps
STAGES=5 with wgmma_arrive_once+wait<1>; more stages but worse than STAGES=4 - perhaps SMEM pressure or non-power-of-2 stages hurts scheduler
Phase2: #pragma unroll 4 on consumer K-loop; reduces branch overhead and exposes ILP across K-tile boundaries, improving instruction scheduling; +13.9 TFLOPS breakthrough
warpgroup_wait<2> + unroll128: deeper WGMMA pipelining - no gain over iter042 at 408.8T
Swizzled tile ordering (block processes consecutive tiles) - WORSE due to DRAM access pattern degradation and load imbalance

bottleneck: tile_boundary_waste: N=512 not divisible by WARP_N=96, 6th partial tile (only 32/96 valid columns) causes severe load imbalance and TMA waste
idea: SASS reverse-engineering: cuBLAS uses WARP_N=96 (HGMMA.64x96x16) to save 16 accum regs. Incompatible with N=512

bottleneck: barrier_parity_bug: raw mbarrier parity tracking wrong; producer never stalls; pipeline desync
idea: Replace cuda::barrier wait() with raw PTX mbarrier spin-wait to eliminate NANOSLEEP. Correct result but only 238T due to incorrect parity tracking

bottleneck: A-matrix bandwidth limit and cuBLAS CGA-multicast architecture gap
idea: L2 persist for B matrix: no gain. TBC multicast: 200T. Compilation key: direct nvcc -Xptxas joined

bottleneck: Late empty.arrive() was delaying producer TMA start
idea: Early empty.arrive before warpgroup_wait<1> for earlier producer unblocking, +0.4T gain

bottleneck: All micro-opts hurt or neutral vs iter052 - stages3 199T mio starves, unroll256 388T icache, cta128 258T extra overhead, prearrive broke unroll
idea: Exhausted local micro-opts: best remains iter052 409.2T. Need deeper architectural change.
